insight: When opening with Conduit in a set with Cynestra, buying Wall early forces suboptimal decisions and delays Animus acquisition. Avoid early Conduit if possible; instead float energy on turn 4 and delay Conduit to reach Animus earlier.
author: spiritfryer
context: Post-game analysis of a set with Cynestra and Brooder discussing opening efficiency.

insight: Cynestra performs poorly against Matrix and Tarsier-heavy strategies. Double Animus opening avoids both Cynestra and Brooder by going Animus first.
author: spiritfryer, TheTrumpWall
context: Debate over single vs. double Animus in a set with Cynestra, Brooder, and Matrix.

insight: Proto Doom effectively counters Asteri Venge by forcing the opponent's red tech into Proto absorb instead of allowing Venge to be aggressive, reducing Venge's value.
author: silentslayers, spiritfryer
context: Discussion of Doom, Venge, and Proto interactions in a complex set.

insight: Proto is the best absorber in a set with Asteri and Iceblade; committing to second Animus to unlock Blood Pact + Proto is generally worse than second Asteri due to second Asteri's power, even though it would reduce Forcefield needs.
author: spiritfryer, masn6811
context: Strategic analysis of whether double Animus or double Asteri is better in a specific set.

insight: Iceblade loses almost always against green Blood Pact strategies. Even when a set appears to support Iceblade, green-based units like Asteri beat it, making Iceblade BP rarely viable.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of Iceblade matchups in sets with strong green alternatives.

insight: Against Iceblade, buying two Asteri early while taking damage is often correct; if opponent doesn't defend the Asteri, you kill drones and win the exchange; if they do defend, 6 damage is enough to kill Iceblade.
author: masn6811
context: Explanation of how green BP strategies beat Iceblade.

insight: Steelsplitter is almost always better than Forcefield as an absorber because a 3 HP attacker blocking is superior to a 2 HP fragile wall. The only niche exception is with Resophore, where Forcefield can generate Tarsier vigilant units.
author: velizar_, shadourow, silentslayers
context: Debate over whether Forcefield or Steelsplitter is better for absorption.

insight: Lifespan mechanics work similar to Magic: The Gathering upkeep—a unit with 1 lifespan remaining applies its start-of-turn effect, then dies at the beginning of its owner's next turn.
author: shadourow, velizar_
context: Clarification of how lifespan mechanics work when a unit has 1 remaining lifespan.

insight: Floating excessive green (5+) early for multiple Forcefield turns is usually worse than spending green on early Forcefields and buying Steelsplitters instead due to inflation effects making it suboptimal to save green.
author: shadourow, amalloy
context: Discussion of whether to float green for future defense or spend it early.

insight: Venge Cannon is efficient at dodging absorb (especially with Infusion support), turning gold economy into sword economy. It is extremely powerful when the opponent cannot efficiently spend blue on absorb.
author: silentslayers, masn6811
context: Analysis of Venge value in dodging absorb mechanics.

insight: Whether to click Venge or buy more depends on inflation and tempo needs: at high inflation, clicking Venge is better; at lower inflation, buying additional Venges may be correct. Decision timing is often felt rather than calculated.
author: masn6811, silentslayers
context: Discussion of optimal Venge usage frequency.

insight: Galvani Drone is severely overpowered in balanced event format. It should cost buildtime 2 instead of buildtime 1 to prevent using it as a drone replacement and enable absorb strategies.
author: masn6811
context: Criticism of Galvani Drone's balance in event format.

insight: Antima Comet is too efficient and forces uncertain decisions between buying attack and buying defense. Changing cost to 2GBB or 9GBBR with buildtime 3 would increase commitment and decision clarity.
author: masn6811, silentslayers
context: Balance discussion of Antima Comet cost and commit

insight: Savior needs a commitment cost (like Resophore treatment) to force players to play real games before it arrives. Alternative: make Savior produce Infusions or Stamina units instead of Plasma, or make Plasma units Prompt.
author: masn6811, pikachumemes, megasupp
context: Design suggestions for Savior balance.

insight: Centurion is typically not buyable if opponent threatens to attack with Tatsu Nullifier, Amorphous Aggregate, or Vai Mauronax due to cheap small red attackers forcing fast games.
author: silentslayers
context: Discussion of units that make Centurion impossible to reach.

insight: Buying Centurion when Energy Matrix is already in play is inefficient. Matrix provides a 5 HP absorber, so Centurion becomes essentially 6 HP and 2 attack per turn, which is worse than buying two Steelsplitters, Gauss Cannon, and keeping leftover resources.
author: velizar_
context: Cost-benefit analysis of Centurion vs. other options when Matrix exists.

insight: Centurion's 6 prompt HP is equivalent to 6 prompt Engineers in defense value. This makes Centurion equivalent to buying two Walls (10BB) plus 8GGR for 2 attack, achieving Shadowfang-level attack efficiency.
author: pikachumemes, velizar_
context: Efficiency analysis of Centurion's cost-to-benefit ratio.

insight: Centurion is skippable less often than commonly assumed but is still correct to skip in approximately 7-10% of games. It is the correct purchase more than 90% of the time.
author: masn6811
context: Meta assessment of Centurion's overall viability.

insight: In fast sets with Drake, Lancetooth, and Smorcus, P2 should consider aggressive openings like float 4 into BBD into Drake, or Smorcus opening (DA into Smorcus+DD) rather than single Iceblade builds.
author: silentslayers
context: Discussion of aggressive P2 opening strategies in a fast set.

insight: Iceblade builds are beatable when they don't all-in by buying multiple Iceblades at once. Single Iceblade per turn provides weak pressure and is easily handled by blue or green Blood Pact strategies.
author: amalloy, silentslayers
context: Evaluation of Iceblade's effectiveness as a pressure unit.

insight: Odd-numbered health defenders are strong against Iceblade because Iceblade freezes units without killing them. Walls (2 HP, fragile) are essential defense against Iceblade due to their odd breakpoint.
author: silentslayers
context: Explanation of defender selection against Iceblade.

insight: Green (Forcefield) is rarely buyable until the very end of fast sets with Drake and Lancetooth because the tempo pressure is too high. Units like Nivos have limited impact because they only freeze walls and Lancetooth.
author: silentslayers
context: Analysis of green tech's viability in fast-paced sets.
